steps: The cruising speed varies between 175 mph and 195 mph. "Varies between" means the speed is at least the lower value and at most the upper value: $s \geq 175$ and $s \leq 195$.
Combine both conditions into a single compound inequality: $$175 \leq s \leq 195$$

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $s \leq 20$ — Imposes a cap of 20 mph with no lower bound; unrelated to the given range. Eliminated.
  • B: $s \leq 175$ — Only an upper bound of 175 mph; ignores the upper limit of 195 mph and allows values below 175. Eliminated.
  • C: $s \leq 195$ — Only an upper bound of 195 mph; ignores the lower limit of 175 mph. Eliminated.
  • D: $175 \leq s \leq 195$ — Requires $s$ to be at least 175 and at most 195, matching both bounds exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
